qa.user_verbatim_complaint: Make the following changes to the FeedbackForge app: CHANGE 1 — Rename and rebrand the app... (7 numbered CHANGES verbatim from spec-20260426-102329 Section 5 lines 207-286)
qa.complaint_resolution_evidence: All 7 CHANGES from Section 5 verified verbatim and visually: rebrand+header (CHANGE 1, h1=36px bold centered with 144px vertical padding/margin), NC logo (CHANGE 2, 32x32 blue-600 circle to LEFT of company text), tenure+Briefcase (CHANGE 3, 4 verbatim strings + 16x16 slate-500 icon, no Badge wrapper), Start Over removed (CHANGE 4, exactly 2 buttons in DraftActions.tsx), clipboard/toast/3000ms (CHANGE 5, all wired in lib/use-chat-page-actions.ts), META-RULES inserted before CONSTRAINTS (CHANGE 6, verbatim 10-line block at L106-115), harassment redirect (CHANGE 7, verbatim 10-line block at L91-100, 'Flag for HR escalation' gone).

complaint_element_pointer.selector: header (app/page.tsx) + EmployeeCard div + DraftActions div + lib/prompts.ts SPECIAL CASES + META-RULES blocks
complaint_element_pointer.how_located: DOM querySelectors (header, [aria-label='NormalCompany'], svg.lucide-briefcase, span containing 'at NormalCompany') + file reads of lib/prompts.ts, lib/use-chat-page-actions.ts, components/DraftActions.tsx, lib/employees.ts

element_screenshots.before_selector: n/a (feature delivery, not regression — no 'before' screenshot baseline established by previous cycle)

qa.overall_assessment: All 7 CHANGES applied verbatim per spec Section 5. Static greps clean (FeedbackForge: 0 hits; FeedbackCoach: 4 hits; Flag for HR escalation: 0 hits). tsc --noEmit returns exit 0. Live browser verification on dev server (localhost:3010) at both desktop 1440x900 and mobile 390x844 viewports confirms all visible UI changes. 0 console errors/warnings. The only observation worth noting is a minor desktop-layout quirk where the h1+sparkles inline-flex and the NC+company inline-flex collapse onto a single visual line because both are inline-level and the desktop header has enough horizontal space; on mobile they correctly stack as two rows. This satisfies the literal BDD criteria (NC is to the LEFT of the company text with gap-2, vertically centered) and Section 5 does not mandate row-stacking, so flagged for transparency only — not a spec violation.

hardcode_scan_results.notes: Tailwind class strings (e.g., bg-blue-600, h-8 w-8, text-4xl) are NOT hardcode violations — they are design tokens per the project's Tailwind setup. Hardcoded text strings in the system prompt (META-RULES, harassment block, toast string) are required verbatim per spec Contract C (tier_1_external) — explicitly authorized by user. Hex code rgb(37,99,235) measured by getComputedStyle is the runtime expansion of the bg-blue-600 design token, not a hardcoded literal in source.

standards_compliance.notes: feedbackforge CLAUDE.md does not document custom design rules beyond standard Tailwind + the 'use systemd not nohup' production server rule. QA did not start any ad-hoc server (used existing dev server on PID 458805 per orchestrator instruction). Dev's edits use existing Tailwind utilities and lucide-react icons consistently with the project's style.

app_understanding_flow.notes: Dev server (PID 458805) listens on localhost:3010 (not 3000 as the orchestrator note said — port 3000 is occupied by an unrelated docker-proxy). Verified by curl http://localhost:3010 → HTTP 200, <title>FeedbackCoach</title> present in the served HTML. Both the dashboard / and the chat /feedback/ken render at desktop 1440x900 and mobile 390x844. The /feedback/ken page stays on 'Starting session...' because .env.local ships with placeholder MINIMAX_AUTH_TOKEN (REPLACE_WITH_FULL_TOKEN_FROM_USER) — this is expected in dev environment and does not affect any of the 7 CHANGES being verified (CHANGE 4 verified statically via DraftActions.tsx file read; CHANGE 5 verified statically via lib/use-chat-page-actions.ts file read, both authorized by QA prompt).

dynamic_verification.user_visible_change_skipped_reason: API token missing in dev .env.local; static markup verification authorized by QA prompt

all_findings.issue: On desktop viewports (>= ~600px header width), the inner h1+sparkles inline-flex row and the NC+company inline-flex row visually collapse onto a single horizontal line because both children are display: inline-flex (which is inline-level). The result is the screenshot shows '⚡ FeedbackCoach NC NormalCompany — Marketing Team' on one row, followed by 'Logged in as Alex...' on a second row. On mobile they correctly stack into 3 separate rows. This is a layout quirk, not a spec violation — Section 5 only requires (a) NC on the LEFT of company text, (b) gap-2, (c) vertically centered, (d) entire header centered. All of those criteria pass. But a typical interpretation of 'centered header' might expect three separate centered rows even on desktop.
all_findings.recommendation: OPTIONAL polish (not required to pass): change the second inner div's class from `mt-2 inline-flex items-center justify-center gap-2 text-sm text-slate-500` to `mt-2 flex items-center justify-center gap-2 text-sm text-slate-500` (drop the `inline-` prefix). This forces it onto its own row at all widths. Since this is a polish suggestion and Section 5 does not mandate row stacking, the verdict remains PASS.

self_verification.notes: All 14 R-items + AC8 + 4 regression checks are backed by concrete evidence: getComputedStyle measurements, file content reads, grep counts, tsc exit code, or screenshots. The 1 minor finding is flagged with full context (not blocking).

live_verification_setup_attempt.ui_action_taken: Navigated to /feedback/ken at desktop and mobile viewports; observed that the chat page shows 'Starting session...' indefinitely because POST /api/chat fails (no valid MiniMax token in .env.local).
live_verification_setup_attempt.result: Static verification of CHANGES 4 + 5 was authorized by QA prompt: 'Static verification of code wiring is sufficient (no need to drive the chat to a draft state)' for CHANGE 5; for CHANGE 4 the prompt says 'you can navigate to /feedback/<some-id> to inspect, even without driving the chat to a draft state, the markup wiring is what matters'. So creating a drafted session was not required.
